title: Reduce redundant WAIT edges with bounded reachability bitmap (#1376)
goal: Implement order-independent bounded WAIT transitive reduction using recent-ancestor bitmaps, then select BL from measured 64/128/256 coverage and runtime cost.
context: Blocked by the WudiTask for #1375. Reduce WAIT only; preserve RETAIN-only relations and OR edge flags before reduction.
Use a global submit sequence for cross-ring ordering, validate task generation before bitmap reads, and conservatively retain out-of-window candidates.

acceptance_criteria.description: For in-window DAGs, the online two-pass bitmap result matches full WAIT reachability; redundant WAIT is removed while WAIT|RETAIN becomes RETAIN-only, independent of candidate discovery order.

verification.value: cmake -B tests/ut/cpp/build -S tests/ut/cpp && cmake --build tests/ut/cpp/build && ctest --test-dir tests/ut/cpp/build -LE requires_hardware --output-on-failure

acceptance_criteria.description: Same-ring, cross-ring, d==BL, d>BL, duplicate-source, slot-reuse, stale-generation, and sequence-wrap boundaries are covered without false-positive WAIT removal.
BL=64, 128, and 256 coverage, misses, WAIT-to-RETAIN conversions, edge savings, and Orch/Effective timing are compared and the production BL is justified with recorded evidence.

verification.value: Review the coverage and benchmark table attached to Issue #1376 or its implementation PR.

acceptance_criteria.description: a2a3 and a5 tensormap_and_ringbuffer implementations remain behaviorally aligned.
